nodes.name: Fetch One Pending Tour
nodes.notes: GROQ now ends with [0] so exactly one tour (or null) comes back per execution -> guarantees one video per run.

nodes.name: Tour Found?
nodes.notes: Stops the run cleanly when no unprocessed tours remain instead of erroring downstream.

messageValues.message:
=You are a luxury travel copywriter for "Goldmoon Egypt Tours". Write captivating, minimalist marketing copy for a 10-second vertical promotional video.

STRICT RULES:
1. Each text_scene value: max 5 words, max 60 characters, English only (no Arabic or other scripts), no emojis.
2. Output ONLY a single valid JSON object - no markdown, no code fences, no explanations.
3. music_mood MUST be exactly one of: "desert_ambient", "luxury_chill", "cinematic_epic" (no other values).

REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT:
{
  "video_title": "short punchy title, max 60 chars",
  "description": "1-2 sentence SEO description with a call to action",
  "tags": ["Tag1", "Tag2", "Tag3", "Tag4", "Tag5"],
  "text_scene_1": "...",
  "text_scene_2": "...",
  "text_scene_3": "...",
  "text_scene_4": "...",
  "music_mood": "desert_ambient|luxury_chill|cinematic_epic"
}

nodes.name: Basic LLM Chain
nodes.notes: Now runs on the single tour object from 'Fetch One Pending Tour' (via Tour Found? true branch), not on the raw multi-tour array. Schema unified with what 'Build Render Payload' actually reads (text_scene_1..4 + music_mood).

nodes.name: Build Render Payload
nodes.notes: Rewritten to output {scenes, scene_texts, bg_music, website_url} matching VideoRequest in main.py. The old version sent image_urls/text_scene_1..4/zoom_speed/transition_type, none of which match the API schema, so /render was returning 422.

nodes.name: Render Video
nodes.notes: Response is the raw MP4 (binary property 'data'), not JSON. There is no video_url on this node's output - that's why the next node uploads the binary to Sanity to obtain a real URL.

nodes.name: Upload Video to Sanity
nodes.notes: NEW node. Uploads the rendered MP4 binary to Sanity's file-asset endpoint so we get back a real hosted URL (document.url) to store on the tour. Without this step there is nothing to put in video_url.

nodes.name: Mark Tour Processed
nodes.notes: video_url now comes from the Sanity upload response (document.url), not from Render Video's output (which never had a URL field).
